blurb: A Washington Post analysis of a national poll on climate beliefs, debunking recycling and other feel-good habits as measures of real climate progress and pointing instead toward the systemic changes that actually matter.
A report examining how climate change disproportionately threatens the health, culture, and ancestral heritage of Latino communities in the U.S.

title_clean: Climate Beacon Newsroom Initiative: A Story of Nine Newsrooms' Solutions Journalism Transformation
blurb: An ArcGIS StoryMap documenting the Climate Beacon Newsroom Initiative, in which nine newsrooms transformed their coverage to center solutions journalism on the global climate crisis.
ambiguous_note: Title is a mangled StoryMap header with an author credit tacked on; cleaned for readability but exact framing of the piece is inferred from title and platform alone.

blurb: An exploration of litter's overlooked psychological toll, arguing that visible environmental degradation carries a quiet mental health cost alongside its ecological one.

title_clean: Only 18% of the Global Land Area Needed for Human Well-Being and Biodiversity Is Currently Protected
blurb: A ScienceDaily summary of research finding that only 18% of the global land area required to sustain both human well-being and biodiversity currently falls under protection.

title_clean: Sustainable Food Chains for Global Well-Being
blurb: A profile of a Banting-Vanier scholar's research into redesigning food systems so they support both ecological health and human well-being.
An HR Executive piece arguing that inclusive, sustainability-minded workplace cultures -- not perks or policy alone -- are the strongest driver of organizational performance.
A Pachamama Alliance essay tracing how social justice and environmental justice are inseparable, arguing that neither can be won without the other.
A Reasons to Be Cheerful account of how Medellin, Colombia dramatically cooled its urban core in just three years by planting green corridors along its streets and waterways.

title_clean: See How Much Nature You Have Access to in Your Neighborhood and City
blurb: An interactive Washington Post tool letting readers see, block by block, how much accessible nature exists in their own neighborhood and city.
A WHO policy brief making the case for integrating mental health considerations directly into climate change policy and response.

title_clean: The Financial Well-Being of Fruit Farmers in Chile and Tunisia Depends More on Social and Geographical Factors Than on Climate Change
blurb: A study in Communications Earth & Environment finding that fruit farmers' financial well-being in Chile and Tunisia hinges more on social and geographic circumstances than on climate change itself.
A Nature Mental Health article making the case for embedding mental health expertise and concerns directly into global climate negotiations.
An interactive CDC/ATSDR mapping tool letting users explore environmental justice burden data -- pollution, social vulnerability, health outcomes -- for any U.S. location.

title_clean: Environmental Justice Index: Place and Health
blurb: The CDC/ATSDR's Environmental Justice Index, a national framework ranking U.S. communities by their cumulative environmental, social, and health burdens.
A conversation with climate psychology researcher Dr. Panu Pihkala unpacking the sources and shapes of climate anxiety.
A Guardian report on actuaries' warning that unchecked climate shocks could shrink the global economy by as much as 50% between 2070 and 2090.
